title: Service Outcomes, Boundaries, and Server Lifecycle · Go Semantics Case: Lifecycle Evidence Ownership
summary: A civic incident API has endpoints but no stated user outcome, ownership boundary, startup contract, dependency readiness, shutdown policy, or evidence connecting a request to durable effect. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo lifecycle evidence ownership increment in service outcome, boundary, and lifecycle map. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Assign startup, request, dependency, shutdown, rollback, alert, and data-recovery evidence to named owners and done conditions.
Validate configuration, bind the intended listener, initialize dependencies, expose liveness separately from readiness, and fail startup atomically.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Lifecycle Evidence Ownership · Theory Lifecycle Evidence Ownership
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 3435 in a partner API with an outdated generated client. Build deterministic Go evidence to separate listener liveness, dependency readiness, and draining state; then repeat the request with a different method and retain status plus durable-effect evidence. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Lifecycle Evidence Ownership contract for the supplied data.
